messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BioC2026 Call for Abstracts is Closing Soon
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] BioC2026 Call for Abstracts is Closing Soon
messages.body_plain:
Just a quick reminder that the Call for Abstracts for BioC2026 is currently open. Submit abstracts for workshops, posters, package demos, and short talks.

 Submission deadline: 13th March 2026

?Submit your abstract here: https://openreview.net/group?id=bioconductor.org/BioC/2026/Conference#tab-recent-activity

??Please note that the OpenReview submission system requires all submitters to create an account at least two weeks before the abstract deadline. Please register early to avoid any last-minute issues.

Find more information here: https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/

Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

GENESIS is failing on the mac builder kjohnson3
<https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/GENESIS/kjohnson3-buildsrc.html>
with an error that I don't understand. It is passing on linux, as well as
every system (including macOS) on R universe
<https://bioc.r-universe.dev/GENESIS#checktable>. I don't know how to
proceed here, as I can't reproduce the error. Can anyone offer guidance? Is
there a chance that passing the R universe builds will be sufficient for
inclusion in the next Bioonductor release?

thanks,
Stephanie Gogarten

-- 

Stephanie M. Gogarten, PhD

Senior Research Scientist / Genetic Analysis Center
<https://www.biostat.washington.edu/research/centers/gac>

Department of Biostatistics <https://www.biostat.washington.edu/>

UNIVERSITY OF WASHINGTON



UW Tower Box 359461

4333 Brooklyn Ave NE, Seattle, WA 98195

206.221.0757

sdmorris at uw.edu

she/her/hers

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Hi,

GENESIS is failing on the mac builder kjohnson3
<https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/GENESIS/kjohnson3-buildsrc.html>
with an error that I don't understand. It is passing on l

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3
messages.body_plain:
Dear Stephanie Gogarten,

? Fri, 6 Mar 2026 16:22:27 -0800
Stephanie Gogarten <sdmorris at uw.edu> ?????:

> GENESIS is failing on the mac builder kjohnson3
> <https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/GENESIS/kjohnson3-buildsrc.html>
> with an error that I don't understand.

>> Error in `[.data.table`:
>> ! attempt access index 3/3 in VECTOR_ELT

>> 16. x[, `:=`("allele.index", seq_len(.N)), by = "variant.id"]
>> 17. data.table:::`[.data.table`(...)

The macOS builder 'kjohnson3' has data.table version 1.17.8 installed
[*]. The 1.17.x series was increasingly incompatible with R-devel,
which we only fixed in 1.18.0 (and then fixed again in 1.18.2.1). This
is one of those problems:
https://github.com/Rdatatable/data.table/issues/7486

Installing data.table 1.18.2.1 from CRAN on kjohnson3 should fix the
problem.

-- 
Best regards,
Ivan

[*]
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/kjohnson3-R-instpkgs.html

Hello,

Hope you are well!

I noticed that magick has a build error on kjohnson3. This is impacting the
SpatialExperiment package, which is a dependency of one of my packages,
spoon. It looks like the nebbiolo1 builder is fine. Do you know why this is
happening?

Thanks!
Kinnary

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] March Developer Forum
messages.body_plain:
Hi folks,


There is an upcoming Bioconductor Developer Forum scheduled for Thursday March 26th at 4pm GMT. The goal of the forum is to provide a venue for discussing technical, scientific, and infrastructure topics related to Bioconductor. The typical Forum structure is intended to include two short talks and plenty of time for healthy discussion.


The upcoming forum has a single talk scheduled:

Michael Lawrence - S7 Classes


The Forum is currently scheduled for 4pm GMT on the last Thursday of every month. If you have any questions or would like to present a topic (topic talks should range from 5-15 minutes to ensure there is room for healthy discussion) at a future Forum, please get in touch with me. I look forward to seeing you there! Zoom information is provided in the link below.


Cheers,

Nick

Registration link:
https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/RWWBRMnjSi-3NHRE24wPKQ


Nicholas Cooley, PhD (he/him)
Developer Engagement Lead
Bioconductor / University of Limerick






	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] The BioC2026 Call for Abstracts deadline has been extended
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] The BioC2026 Call for Abstracts deadline has been extended
messages.body_plain:
There is now more time to share your work with the Bioconductor community. Abstracts are welcome in the following categories:

  *
Short talks: 10-minute presentations followed by Q&A, focused on packages, research questions, or technology that motivates software development.
  *
Posters: Printed posters presented during the poster session.
  *
Workshops: 1.5-hour hands-on, interactive sessions demonstrating a software package or workflow.
  *
Package demos: Interactive, hands-on software demonstrations

 New submission deadline: 20th March 2026

 Submit your abstract here: https://openreview.net/group?id=bioconductor.org/BioC/2026/Conference#tab-recent-activity

Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3
messages.body_plain:
Kjohnson3 has the updated data.table.  The issue is the missing reverse dependencies.  It looks like its stemming from the error in gdsfmt, that prevents the other packages from being available. When gdsfmt is available this should resolve.



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor package developers,

Thank you for using rworkflows and for your continued support.

Unfortunately, CRAN has deprecated the package due to unresolved errors,
despite no errors or warnings being reported in their logs. We are actively
working to have the package reinstated, but CRAN has not been responsive
over the past few weeks. As a result, we have initiated a resubmission.

As a temporary measure, we recommend removing rworkflows from Suggests.
GitHub Actions workflows will remain unaffected. The only change is that
developers will need to manually install rworkflows from GitHub in order to
knit the README (if rworkflows functions are used here). While not ideal,
this does not impact package functionality and can be reverted once
rworkflows is restored on CRAN.

We appreciate your patience and understanding, and we will keep you updated
on any progress.

Kind regards,

Hiru

--

*Hiranyamaya (Hiru) Dash*
Research Assistant in Computational Genomics
UK Dementia Research Institute at Imperial College London

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Dear Bioconductor package developers,

Thank you for using rworkflows and for your continued support.

Unfortunately, CRAN has deprecated the package due to unresolved errors,
despite no errors or war

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.body_plain:
Thanks for the update.

Just in case, there is indeed a CRAN check NOTE. On
https://cran-archive.r-project.org/web/checks/2026/2026-03-03_check_results_rworkflows.html,
there's a link to 'donttest' check logs:

https://www.stats.ox.ac.uk/pub/bdr/donttest/rworkflows.out

which reports:

* checking for new files in some other directories ... NOTE
Found the following files/directories:
  ?~/.cache/R/rworkflows?
  ?~/.cache/R/rworkflows/versions-manifest.json?

I think the rule is that unless those existed before R CMD check started,
they shouldn't be there afterwards, i.e. running checks shouldn't leave
anything behind.

You can manually check for this, but it's probably easier to set something
like

Sys.setenv(XDG_CACHE_HOME = tempdir())

at the beginning of your test scripts.

Henrik

On Fri, Mar 20, 2026, 05:16 Hiranyamaya Dash <hdash.work at gmail.com> wrote:

> Dear Bioconductor package developers,
>
> Thank you for using rworkflows and for your continued support.
>
> Unfortunately, CRAN has deprecated the package due to unresolved errors,
> despite no errors or warnings being reported in their logs. We are actively
> working to have the package reinstated, but CRAN has not been responsive
> over the past few weeks. As a result, we have initiated a resubmission.
>
> As a temporary measure, we recommend removing rworkflows from Suggests.
> GitHub Actions workflows will remain unaffected. The only change is that
> developers will need to manually install rworkflows from GitHub in order to
> knit the README (if rworkflows functions are used here). While not ideal,
> this does not impact package functionality and can be reverted once
> rworkflows is restored on CRAN.
>
> We appreciate your patience and understanding, and we will keep you updated
> on any progress.
>
> Kind regards,
>
> Hiru
>
> --
>
> *Hiranyamaya (Hiru) Dash*
> Research Assistant in Computational Genomics
> UK Dementia Research Institute at Imperial College London
>
>         [[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>
> _______________________________________________
> Bioc-devel at r-project.org mailing list
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/bioc-devel
>

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.body_plain:
Dear Henrik,

Thank you for bringing this to my attention.

This NOTE has since been addressed. All instances of cache_dir have been updated to use tempdir(), ensuring the cache is session-scoped.

However, it is unclear whether this NOTE was the cause of the deprecation.

Kind regards,
Hiru

From: Henrik Bengtsson <henrik.bengtsson at gmail.com>
Date: Friday, 20 March 2026 at 2:13?pm
To: Hiranyamaya Dash <hdash.work at gmail.com>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>, Skene, Nathan G <n.skene at imperial.ac.uk>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps

Thanks for the update.

Just in case, there is indeed a CRAN check NOTE. On https://cran-archive.r-project.org/web/checks/2026/2026-03-03_check_results_rworkflows.html, there's a link to 'donttest' check logs:

https://www.stats.ox.ac.uk/pub/bdr/donttest/rworkflows.out

which reports:

* checking for new files in some other directories ... NOTE
Found the following files/directories:
  ?~/.cache/R/rworkflows?
  ?~/.cache/R/rworkflows/versions-manifest.json?

I think the rule is that unless those existed before R CMD check started, they shouldn't be there afterwards, i.e. running checks shouldn't leave anything behind.

You can manually check for this, but it's probably easier to set something like

Sys.setenv(XDG_CACHE_HOME = tempdir())

at the beginning of your test scripts.

Henrik

On Fri, Mar 20, 2026, 05:16 Hiranyamaya Dash <hdash.work at gmail.com<mailto:hdash.work at gmail.com>> wrote:
Dear Bioconductor package developers,

Thank you for using rworkflows and for your continued support.

Unfortunately, CRAN has deprecated the package due to unresolved errors,
despite no errors or warnings being reported in their logs. We are actively
working to have the package reinstated, but CRAN has not been responsive
over the past few weeks. As a result, we have initiated a resubmission.

As a temporary measure, we recommend removing rworkflows from Suggests.
GitHub Actions workflows will remain unaffected. The only change is that
developers will need to manually install rworkflows from GitHub in order to
knit the README (if rworkflows functions are used here). While not ideal,
this does not impact package functionality and can be reverted once
rworkflows is restored on CRAN.

We appreciate your patience and understanding, and we will keep you updated
on any progress.

Kind regards,

Hiru

--

*Hiranyamaya (Hiru) Dash*
Research Assistant in Computational Genomics
UK Dementia Research Institute at Imperial College London

        [[alternative HTML version deleted]]

? Fri, 20 Mar 2026 14:21:29 +0000
"Hiranyamaya Dash (Hiru)" <hdash.work at gmail.com> ?????:

> However, it is unclear whether this NOTE was the cause of the
> deprecation.

That must have been the case:

tools:::CRAN_package_issues() |> subset(Package == 'rworkflows') |>
tail(1)
#               ID    Package                Date From     Before
# 22099 2026.15520 rworkflows 2026-02-16 10:20:07  BDR 2026-03-02
#                   Title Label Info
# 22099 Additional_issues  <NA> <NA>

Did an e-mail from around 16th of February land in the spam folder?

In the future, it may help to set up a periodic check to warn in case
subset(tools::CRAN_package_db(), Package == 'rworkflows', 'Deadline')
stops being NA.

-- 
Best regards,
Ivan

Andres Wokaty (they/them)

Waldron Lab at CUNY SPH
Bioconductor Core Team

Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] help with GENESIS failing build on kjohnson3

Dear Stephanie Gogarten,

? Fri, 6 Mar 2026 16:22:27 -0800
Stephanie Gogarten <sdmorris at uw.edu> ?????:

> GENESIS is failing on the mac builder kjohnson3
> <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://secure-web.cisco.com/1uxWopRUKGxrKty-9E3pPdLzEQDXKQTYACla6wKF6BgqgXazR2N4PJupr5lMQ37fW6Y_FxJoZnOOKvf4xP-nwcdwgXCoJXkYjHYTQE2oAVo1gtbsP9AvqgZcgyLHzP1-V97g0GVH72m2Set0RcA3008f2ZATAfEX0B2T5_NS9yDYAu8w90a7HObdxTRCX05O7IjQB0NHSa-T7zUZzF5bzC-rGOMIAzaPoMTPnG5dJXtkZt-hyX2f1AjcjW22J2O-P8qUejuvGIxoGkWCcypW7IWm_jrHu1CL3U_hohOG3Et41nNn0lSkyTDubgwEHmEG40qVCY8DHdbgaZEurEy_Fqw/https*3A*2F*2Fbioconductor.org*2FcheckResults*2Fdevel*2Fbioc-LATEST*2FGENESIS*2Fkjohnson3-buildsrc.html__;JSUlJSUlJSU!!LRXxDv2l!UDncoDbn75OrJg6Ol-KEupgwvw2LeJYjPXLBqji5AU2LzYA_aDzfZzuGV3eDyhtz7N47LHjtkitGSP8hk1ztCk90dklnFuXU5Q$ >
> with an error that I don't understand.

>> Error in `[.data.table`:
>> ! attempt access index 3/3 in VECTOR_ELT

>> 16. x[, `:=`("allele.index", seq_len(.N)), by = "variant.id"]
>> 17. data.table:::`[.data.table`(...)

The macOS builder 'kjohnson3' has data.table version 1.17.8 installed
[*]. The 1.17.x series was increasingly incompatible with R-devel,
which we only fixed in 1.18.0 (and then fixed again in 1.18.2.1). This
is one of those problems:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://secure-web.cisco.com/1chZlsCjLutNlUFn5guBKWRqc-uITeUAaeE3i8QmQHJfxDNa-Aeq2fvwKyVir54tPHIK7B8w6pKg185Yi1UpiHnWTLv2SR6WIduKvGfQoyUMw3Mi1Z3nV2P0jwpzzOx9JpENSqnyCZATW2g5OUnXrZUT6Lv19tkYctlaZvXFidlsQbgg7bysX8DVrmdZw_5w4ppaLFzEAfMvP6wo2wBh3b3Prkvf3T7QI018XqzIdGXvNNG-Fm9jaXGwN2GslE0eUnh1PdapC-mw0hw6MfdNk6BCc_5w1eYlQbHYNkUOed2iKQ0pcc6AVHt4ImL_h7ZSMkmLdcVR5tCB3nXB6KXE0gA/https*3A*2F*2Fgithub.com*2FRdatatable*2Fdata.table*2Fissues*2F7486__;JSUlJSUlJQ!!LRXxDv2l!UDncoDbn75OrJg6Ol-KEupgwvw2LeJYjPXLBqji5AU2LzYA_aDzfZzuGV3eDyhtz7N47LHjtkitGSP8hk1ztCk90dkm2GjFSUA$

Installing data.table 1.18.2.1 from CRAN on kjohnson3 should fix the
problem.

--
Best regards,
Ivan

[*]
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://secure-web.cisco.com/19NaiPRdzf_7C6PyXW-zg4fi1gs9oDBCmK5BSr-3sRyrAjEJ78V9XI4rBtU1lOwCARBPdNzrfLJB1rZbEc56ePKfvnKAmx6UbShoBVd-WYi8iIhiWh6NwyPofT2chDHCv5HBnqu0CLtT9pdRni9NzZb0r2SnX5QZJmOW3m4owLJq5n_lQCLCXqiEVD8YPmrFSz6idlSnuCn53VZcj95IZduuFhjL0cejiHqVpzJPSi1IRQSLtqOrMcH3H-jLpThz9EeB2w493amXdDYgTmlpyVhd3UjwqJmq64eTu-p8AOEeEWu9aNUocb18usHTnMoZLRbXg9g_pyc23w4hl36OGuA/https*3A*2F*2Fbioconductor.org*2FcheckResults*2Fdevel*2Fbioc-LATEST*2Fkjohnson3-R-instpkgs.html__;JSUlJSUlJQ!!LRXxDv2l!UDncoDbn75OrJg6Ol-KEupgwvw2LeJYjPXLBqji5AU2LzYA_aDzfZzuGV3eDyhtz7N47LHjtkitGSP8hk1ztCk90dkn_blX1Cw$

_______________________________________________

messages.body_snippet:
I've updated kjohnson3 and was able R CMD INSTALL and build gdsfmt. We should see it on the report this weekend or Monday.

Andres Wokaty (they/them)

Waldron Lab at CUNY SPH
Bioconductor Core Team
__

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps
messages.body_plain:
Thank you for the suggestion, Ivan. I will use that function going forward.

At the time, the maintainer was away on leave. As I was not listed as a maintainer, I did not receive any notifications about this.

I wish it were possible to have more than one maintainer!

Kind regards,
Hiru

From: Ivan Krylov <ikrylov at disroot.org>
Date: Friday, 20 March 2026 at 6:40?pm
To: Hiranyamaya Dash (Hiru) <hdash.work at gmail.com>
Cc: Skene, Nathan G <n.skene at imperial.ac.uk>, bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] rworkflows temporary CRAN removal and next steps

? Fri, 20 Mar 2026 14:21:29 +0000
"Hiranyamaya Dash (Hiru)" <hdash.work at gmail.com> ?????:

> However, it is unclear whether this NOTE was the cause of the
> deprecation.

That must have been the case:

tools:::CRAN_package_issues() |> subset(Package == 'rworkflows') |>
tail(1)
#               ID    Package                Date From     Before
# 22099 2026.15520 rworkflows 2026-02-16 10:20:07  BDR 2026-03-02
#                   Title Label Info
# 22099 Additional_issues  <NA> <NA>

Did an e-mail from around 16th of February land in the spam folder?

In the future, it may help to set up a periodic check to warn in case
subset(tools::CRAN_package_db(), Package == 'rworkflows', 'Deadline')
stops being NA.

--
Best regards,
Ivan

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
